attributes.description: Alerts when per-partition `kafka.consumer_group.lag > 10000`. Per-partition lag surfaces hot-spotting and stuck consumers.
attributes.name: [Kafka OTel] High consumer lag per partition

investigation_guide.blob:
## Kafka high consumer lag per partition

### What fired
`kafka.consumer_group.lag > 10,000` on at least one partition.

### Why it matters
Per-partition lag isolates the specific partitions / consumers struggling. Useful for detecting key-skew or specific consumer crashes.

### Triage
1. Look at partition assignment (is one consumer owning the hot partition?).
2. Check recent rebalances in the consumer group.

### Remediation
- Rebalance manually or fix the slow consumer.
- Re-key producer data to distribute load.

### Tuning
- 10,000 per partition is generic; tune to throughput.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-kafkametricsreceiver.otel-*
// Per-partition consumer group lag (offset distance behind log-end)
| WHERE kafka.consumer_group.lag IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_lag = MAX(kafka.consumer_group.lag)
  BY attributes.group, attributes.topic, attributes.partition
// Threshold: lag in messages. Adjust for your throughput (e.g. 10000, 100000)
| WHERE max_lag > 10000
| SORT max_lag DESC
